ZoneTargets.description: One irrigation zone's runtime-adjustable targets — the current intended state, mirroring the controller's per-zone config. A copy of the optimizer-write-rest ZoneTargets shape (same Go DTO backs both), kept local per the self-contained-contract convention.

moisture_low_threshold.description: Irrigation triggers below this soil moisture (VWC, 0–1).

moisture_high_threshold.description: Irrigation stops above this soil moisture (VWC, 0–1).

drain_period_secs.description: Minimum gap between irrigation cycles, to prevent root saturation (seconds).

Setpoints.description: A greenhouse's current target bundle: the global climate setpoints plus per-zone irrigation targets — the platform's intended state, the crop-safe baseline the optimizer refines against. A copy of the optimizer-write-rest Setpoints shape (same Go DTO), kept local per the self-contained-contract convention. Numeric targets only — never a crop.

day_start.description: Start of the day window, local time-of-day HH:MM (24h).

day_end.description: End of the day window, local time-of-day HH:MM (24h).

humidity_low_pct.description: Lower humidity safety bound (%RH). The VPD-derived RH target is clamped to [humidity_low_pct, humidity_high_pct].

humidity_high_pct.description: Upper humidity safety bound (%RH). Clamps the VPD-derived RH target from above.

humidity_deadband_pct.description: Full hysteresis band width around the VPD-derived RH target (%RH).

co2_target_ppm.description: CO₂ enrichment target; injector opens below this, closes when reached (ppm).

co2_vent_interlock_threshold_pct.description: Vent position above which the CO₂ injector is hard-interlocked off (% open).

vpd_target_kpa.description: The vapor-pressure-deficit target (kPa) — the primary humidity control input.

dli_target_mol.description: Daily Light Integral target driving supplemental lighting (mol·m⁻²·day⁻¹).

zones.description: Per-zone irrigation targets, one entry per configured zone.

Bound.description: A crop-safe [min, max] envelope for one scalar climate target — the range the optimizer may refine that target within, never outside (RFC-005). A copy of the frontend-rest Bound shape, kept local per the self-contained-contract convention.

min.description: Lower edge of the crop-safe range.

max.description: Upper edge of the crop-safe range.

ZoneBounds.description: The active stage's crop-safe envelope for the numeric per-zone irrigation targets, applied uniformly to every zone. An absent target means no crop-specific envelope for it; a zone's schedule carries none. A copy of the frontend-rest ZoneBounds shape, kept local per the self-contained-contract convention.

StageBounds.description: The active profile stage's crop-safe envelope: an optional Bound per scalar climate target, plus an optional per-zone irrigation envelope (`zones`). The platform enforces these on optimizer setpoint writes; the optimizer's constraint engine mirrors them so a plan that clears the engine is expected to be accepted (a 422 signals the envelope disagreed — a mid-cycle profile change or contract drift). An absent target means no crop-specific envelope for it. A copy of the frontend-rest StageBounds shape, kept local per the self-contained-contract convention.

CurrentSetpoints.description: The greenhouse's current intended state with its provenance — the crop-safe baseline the optimizer layers refinements over. `targets` is the same bundle the setpoints write path (optimizer-write-rest) accepts and returns; `bounds` is the active stage's crop-safe envelope the optimizer refines within and the platform enforces on write.

source.description: How the current intended state was last set: the optimizer's refined submission, an operator's ad-hoc edit, or a resolved crop profile (platform §5 provenance).

updated_at.description: When the current intended state was last recorded (RFC 3339 UTC, ms).

SummaryBucket.description: One time-bucket's aggregate for a metric: the (min, mean, max) summary the optimizer serializes into LLM context per sensor per hour (RFC-004), instead of raw readings.

bucket_start.description: Start of the bucket (RFC 3339 UTC, ms). Bucket width is the requested `interval`.

min.description: Minimum sampled value in the bucket. Units are the metric's RFC-007 units.

mean.description: Mean sampled value in the bucket.

max.description: Maximum sampled value in the bucket.

count.description: Number of raw samples aggregated into the bucket (0 signals a gap the completeness check reads).

MetricSummarySeries.description: One metric's bucketed history over the window, as (min, mean, max) summaries. zone_id preserves per-zone identity for soil-moisture series; greenhouse-scoped metrics use null.

zone_id.description: Zone identity for zone-scoped series (soil_moisture), or null for greenhouse-scoped metrics.

buckets.description: Summary buckets ordered by bucket_start, one per interval in the window.

ActuatorSnapshot.description: The latest commanded-vs-observed position for one actuator, plus its readback health. Position is a 0–100 number: modulating actuators report their level; pure on/off devices report 0 (off) or 100 (on).

zone_id.description: Zone identity for per-zone actuators such as irrigation_valve, or null for house-level actuators.

observed.description: Observed readback position (0–100), or null when unavailable.

ts.description: Time of this actuator sample (RFC 3339 UTC, ms).

ActuatorHealth.description: Actuator readback health the optimizer input gate reads: ok (tracking), stuck (readback not following commands), no_response (no readback). A non-ok actuator the plan depends on holds the planning cycle.

ControllerMode.description: The controller's operating mode as the platform last observed it, from the system-state snapshot: normal, degraded (a non-critical fault or fallback), or interlock (a safety interlock is active). A degraded controller marks inputs untrusted for planning.

MetricFreshness.description: Per-metric freshness and completeness the input gate reads: the latest sample's age (against max_telemetry_age_minutes) and how many samples the window holds (against min_history_coverage).

zone_id.description: Zone identity for zone-scoped metrics (soil_moisture), or null for greenhouse-scoped metrics.

latest_ts.description: Timestamp of the most recent sample for this metric/scope (RFC 3339 UTC, ms), or null when the window holds no samples.

age_seconds.description: Seconds between latest_ts and the window's `to`, on the greenhouse's (simulated) clock; null when there is no sample. The freshness check compares this against max_telemetry_age_minutes.

sample_count.description: Raw samples for this metric/scope in the window — the completeness signal.

SensorFault.description: An active per-sensor fault the controller published, read by the input gate's sensor-health check. A metric the plan depends on being faulted marks inputs untrusted.

zone_id.description: Zone identity for zone-scoped metrics (soil_moisture), or null for greenhouse-scoped metrics.

kind.description: The fault class, matching the controller's per-sensor fault events.

since.description: When the fault became active (RFC 3339 UTC, ms).

DataQuality.description: The data-quality / freshness signals the optimizer's input gate runs before invoking the digital twin and planner. Each field maps to one gate check: freshness+completeness (freshness[]), sensor health (faults[]), actuator health (on each ActuatorSnapshot), controller mode (controller_mode), and clock mode (time_scale).

time_scale.description: The greenhouse's reported simulation time-scale (speed): 1.0 is real-time. Simulation-only — null on real hardware. The input gate holds the cycle when a simulated greenhouse reports time_scale ≠ 1.0, since the optimizer's wall-clock-paced cadence is out of its operating envelope off 1×.

freshness.description: Per-metric freshness/completeness, one entry per metric/scope the greenhouse reports.

faults.description: Active per-sensor faults; empty when the controller reports none.

PlanningContext.description: One bounded read of a greenhouse's planning context: current setpoints, bucketed telemetry history, latest actuator states, and data-quality/freshness signals — everything the optimizer's Data Access component needs for one planning cycle. History is returned as (min, mean, max) summaries, not raw readings, so the payload stays bounded (RFC-004). greenhouse_id and schema_version back the optimizer's identity-consistency / drift checks.

schema_version.description: The response schema major version — the drift signal the optimizer's identity-consistency check reads (an unrecognized value means the read API or a contract has drifted). Unlike the write contracts, this read contract carries schema_version in-body because the input gate depends on it; it tracks openapi.json info.version's major.

from.description: Start of the returned window (RFC 3339 UTC, ms), resolved server-side against the greenhouse's latest stored (simulated) timestamp.

to.description: End of the returned window (RFC 3339 UTC, ms) — the greenhouse's latest stored (simulated) timestamp. Ages in data_quality are computed against this.

interval.description: The summary bucket width used for the telemetry series (echo of the resolved `interval` query parameter).

telemetry.description: Bucketed sensor history over the window, one entry per metric/scope pair (per-zone soil moisture stays distinct).

actuators.description: Latest actuator states over the window, one entry per actuator/scope pair.
